propositions.content: LLMs exhibit prompt-sensitive output variation: the same query rephrased differently yields substantively different responses.
Human experts similarly vary their advice depending on how questions are framed (anchoring, priming effects documented in cognitive science).
A governance-first substrate can make AI-mediated knowledge management reliably bounded and repairable, even given unpredictable model behavior at the output layer.
Prompt sensitivity is not a fixable bug but a structural property of next-token prediction; governance at the substrate layer addresses the reliability problem that prompt sensitivity creates.
Reliable output requires reliable model training; substrate governance cannot compensate for fundamental model unpredictability.
